label: Day 8 — Morning: Dissemination Planning
description: Creating something is not enough — it needs to reach people. This session equips each national team with a concrete, realistic dissemination plan: who they will share their outputs with, through which channels, by when, and with what message.
objectives: Develop a concrete dissemination plan for each partner country
Identify target audiences and appropriate channels for each context
Craft key messages that make the outputs relevant to different audiences
Establish commitments for post-project follow-up and network maintenance

steps.title: Why dissemination fails
steps.body: Honest conversation: most project outputs sit unseen on Erasmus+ Results Platform. Why? No clear audience, no follow-up plan, no champion. Today we make sure NORTH doesn't become another forgotten PDF.

steps.title: Dissemination Canvas
steps.body: Each team plans their dissemination: Target audiences, Channels, Key messages per audience, Timeline, and who is responsible for each action.

steps.title: Communication pack
steps.body: Teams draft their core social media post about the project — the one they will publish the day after returning home. They also agree on shared hashtags and a common description of the project.

steps.title: Network commitments
steps.body: Each participant makes one specific, measurable commitment: 'By [date], I will [action] to share [output] with [audience].' Written in the interactive tool — shared with the group and followed up by email in 30 days.

facilitatorNote: Make the dissemination canvas as concrete as possible. 'Post on social media' is not a plan. 'Post a reel on our organisation's Instagram on 15 June with the Green Map link, tagging the three local schools we work with' is a plan. Push every team toward this level of specificity.
